mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, leading to concerns about consumer spending and corporate profits, thus predicting negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:unemployment is a lagging indicator of recession
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated and the Federal Reserve responds by raising interest rates (FEDFUNDS), it tightens financial conditions, increasing the cost of capital and reducing corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in 1 month AND CPIAUCSL > 250
failure_condition: If the specified trigger conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust manufacturing activity and corporate growth, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows monthly growth exceeding 0.5%, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which generally supports corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is a primary measure of economic activity
failure_condition: If annual GDPC1 growth exceeds 2%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a weakening labor market and potential economic slowdown, which negatively impacts consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines MoM for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:employment is key for consumer confidence
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpected increases in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) signal a more hawkish monetary policy than anticipated, which can shock markets by increasing discount rates and tightening liquidity, leading to immediate negative S&P 500 reactions over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25% or more in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:interest rate hikes impact market sentiment
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25% or more in a month, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening demand in the housing sector, which often precedes broader economic slowdowns due to its multiplier effect on related industries, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:housing market is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% quarter-over-quarter, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances.
